# User distilled premium web UI prompt set - Overview

Page type: landing_page
Use cases: premium web UI default aesthetic, high-end landing page, AI product website, SaaS launch page, portfolio editorial page, design agency website
Evidence: summary_only / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-28/premium-web-ui-prompts

Visual traits:
- premium cinematic composition instead of generic templates
- restrained color system with one accent family
- large disciplined typography and strong hierarchy
- material quality through glass, neutral surfaces, media, and precise borders
- subtle staggered motion
- responsive layouts with asymmetry and whitespace

Use when:
- premium web UI default aesthetic
- high-end landing page
- AI product website
- SaaS launch page
- portfolio editorial page
- design agency website

Avoid when:
- generic blue-white SaaS template
- many colors mixed in one page
- thick borders
- heavy dark-mode shadows
- same-size card grids everywhere
- over-centered layouts for every section
- unclear z-index layering
- only adjectives without implementable rules

AI summary: Default premium Web UI rules for agents: avoid generic blue-white SaaS templates; choose one dominant direction such as dark cinematic glass, monochrome SaaS, white editorial luxury, portfolio dark, video-first landing, or premium AI product. Use restrained color, large typography, cinematic media, subtle motion, responsive asymmetry, and implementation-ready component rules.

# User distilled premium web UI prompt set - Layout Rules

Page type: landing_page
Use cases: premium web UI default aesthetic, high-end landing page, AI product website, SaaS launch page, portfolio editorial page, design agency website
Evidence: summary_only / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-28/premium-web-ui-prompts

- Choose one dominant visual direction: dark cinematic glass, black-white neutral SaaS, white editorial luxury, portfolio editorial dark, video-first immersive landing page, or premium AI product/design agency.
- Avoid generic left-text-right-image as the default hero; prefer poster hero, giant staggered type, video-first hero, or product/agency hero.
- Use large whitespace and scale contrast on desktop; stack vertically and keep CTA visible on mobile.
- Use brand design topics only as secondary references; information-insufficient brand topics must not override the premium rules.
- For dark premium UI use near-black backgrounds, white/95 primary text, white/60-80 body text, white/5-15 borders, and white/3-12 surfaces.
- For white editorial UI use white background, black headline/logo/CTA, muted gray nav/body text, and rely on typography, spacing, media, and composition.

AI summary: Default premium Web UI rules for agents: avoid generic blue-white SaaS templates; choose one dominant direction such as dark cinematic glass, monochrome SaaS, white editorial luxury, portfolio dark, video-first landing, or premium AI product. Use restrained color, large typography, cinematic media, subtle motion, responsive asymmetry, and implementation-ready component rules.

# User distilled premium web UI prompt set - Implementation Hints

Page type: landing_page
Use cases: premium web UI default aesthetic, high-end landing page, AI product website, SaaS launch page, portfolio editorial page, design agency website
Evidence: summary_only / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-28/premium-web-ui-prompts

- Use one accent system per page such as purple/pink gradient, blue steel gradient, or monochrome precision.
- Use display/body font pairing such as Instrument Serif italic + Inter, Readex Pro, Inter, or Geist + Inter.
- Hero titles should use line-height 0.8-0.95, letter-spacing -0.02em to -0.05em, and font-weight 400-500.
- Use explicit z-index layering for media: video z-0, overlay z-0/z-5, content z-10/z-20, navbar z-50.
- Use clamp or vw sizes for giant type and check mobile overflow.
- Keep paragraphs concise and avoid filling every empty area.

AI summary: Default premium Web UI rules for agents: avoid generic blue-white SaaS templates; choose one dominant direction such as dark cinematic glass, monochrome SaaS, white editorial luxury, portfolio dark, video-first landing, or premium AI product. content:
# Web-verified Liquid Glass CSS parameter ranges - Layout Rules

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: CSS implementation, React UI, Tailwind UI, premium web app, Liquid Glass component system
Evidence: summary_only / high
Source: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/Properties/backdrop-filter

- Use backdrop-filter only on transparent or partially transparent surfaces; otherwise the blur will not be visible.
- Avoid stacking many glass layers edge-to-edge because seams and optical noise become visible.
- Reserve strong glass for transient surfaces, hero controls, cards, menus, modals, and selected states rather than dense repeated rows.
- Provide fallback solid/translucent colors for unsupported browsers, reduced transparency, battery saver, low-end hardware, and high contrast modes.

AI summary: Liquid Glass CSS parameter ranges: use transparent fills, backdrop-filter blur(12-24px) saturate(140-180%), 1px white rim, large radii, layered outer/inset shadows, gloss pseudo-elements, chromatic caustics, and accessibility/performance fallbacks.

source_name: Web-verified Liquid Glass CSS parameter ranges
content:
# Web-verified Liquid Glass CSS parameter ranges - Implementation Hints

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: CSS implementation, React UI, Tailwind UI, premium web app, Liquid Glass component system
Evidence: summary_only / high
Source: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/Properties/backdrop-filter

- Light glass fill: rgba(255,255,255,0.18) to rgba(255,255,255,0.42); for thicker white glass use 0.45 to 0.68.
- Dark glass fill: rgba(8,10,16,0.56) to rgba(8,10,16,0.78); keep text white or near-white.
- Backdrop filter baseline: blur(12px) saturate(140%) for subtle glass; blur(18px) saturate(160%) for strong frosted glass; blur(24px) saturate(180%) for hero liquid glass.
- Border/rim: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.35) to rgba(255,255,255,0.68); add a lower dark inset edge rgba(20,30,50,0.10) to 0.22 for thickness.

- Use heavier glass blocks for hero cards and lighter glass pills for secondary controls.

# MDN SVG filter turbulence and displacement references - Overview

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: liquid glass interface background, water caustic lighting, refractive product UI, premium modal backdrop, high-end glass controls
Evidence: summary_only / high
Source: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/SVG/Reference/Element/feTurbulence

Visual traits:
- slow refractive water-light caustics behind glass
- thin flowing highlight veins that suggest optical distortion
- subtle cyan, silver, and warm reflected light
- liquid depth without turning the page into an aquarium
- background movement that makes glass edges feel optically active

Use when:
- liquid glass interface background
- water caustic lighting
- refractive product UI
- premium modal backdrop
- high-end glass controls

Avoid when:
- dense data tables
- long reading pages
- forms with many validation messages
- rapid water ripple effects
- high-contrast wave lines under text
- heavy SVG filters over full-screen layers on low-end devices

AI summary: Use water caustics when Liquid Glass needs a more optical, refractive background than a static gradient. Keep flowing light low-opacity, slow, and away from text; implement with SVG turbulence/displacement, low-res canvas noise, or CSS highlight layers, with reduced-motion and static fallbacks.

# MDN SVG filter turbulence and displacement references - Implementation Hints

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: liquid glass interface background, water caustic lighting, refractive product UI, premium modal backdrop, high-end glass controls
Evidence: summary_only / high
Source: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/SVG/Reference/Element/feTurbulence

- SVG baseline: use feTurbulence as a procedural noise source and feDisplacementMap or masked gradients for slow refractive distortion.
- Canvas baseline: render low-resolution flowing noise or line fields, upscale softly, and keep opacity low.
- CSS fallback: layer radial gradients and thin blurred linear highlights with long transform animations.
- Throttle intensity on mobile and avoid animating large blur/filter values every frame.
- Use requestAnimationFrame for canvas animation and stop or pause when the page is hidden.
- Keep caustic animation loops slow, around 24s to 90s, with no visible reset.

AI summary: Use water caustics when Liquid Glass needs a more optical, refractive background than a static gradient. Keep flowing light low-opacity, slow, and away from text; implement with SVG turbulence/displacement, low-res canvas noise, or CSS highlight layers, with reduced-motion and static fallbacks.
